person.name: Fulk FitzWarin I
person.alternateName: Fulk Fitz Warin of Whittington Sir

memoryArtifacts.headline: Fulk Fitz Warin, vitals, wives & children names

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Fulk I FitzWarin ( born 1115, died 1170/1) (alias Fulke, Fouke, FitzWaryn, FitzWarren, Fitz Warine, etc., Latinised to Fulco Filius Warini, "Fulk son of Warin") was a powerful marcher lord seated at Whittington Castle in Shropshire in England on the border with Wales, and also at Alveston in Gloucestershire. His grandson was Fulk III FitzWarin (c. 1160-1258) the subject of the famous mediaeval legend or "ancestral romance" entitled Fouke le Fitz Waryn, himself the grandfather of Fulk V FitzWarin, 1st Baron FitzWarin (1251-1315).

«b»Origins«/b»
Fulk I Fitzwarin was the son of (i.e. in Norman French Fitz, in modern French fils de) "Warin of Metz", the family's earliest known ancestor, thus deemed the family patriarch. Warin of Metz the patriarch is however a "shadowy or mythical figure", about whom little is certain. The later mediaeval romance Fouke le Fitz Waryn gives his name as "Warin de Metz". Whatever his true place of origin it is however generally believed that the head of the Warin family came to England during the reign of William the Conqueror (1066-1087). Neither Warin nor his son Fulk I were during that reign tenants-in-chief, that is to say important vassals or feudal barons, rather the family's grants of lands were obtained from later kings.

«b»Career«/b»
Fulk I FitzWarin was rewarded by King Henry II (1154-1189) for his support of his mother Empress Matilda in her civil war with King Stephen (1135-1154) and conferred to him in 1153 the royal manor of Alveston in Gloucestershire and in 1149 the manor of Whadborough in Leicestershire.

«b»Marriage & progeny«/b»
Fulk I married a lady, Malet Peveral, daughter of Pagan Peveral and produced by her progeny including the following:

1.) Fulk II FitzWarin (fl. 1194), son and heir who held his father's lands following his death in 1170/1.

2.) William de Brightley, younger son, who according to Sir William Pole (d.1635) was granted by his father "in King Henry 2 tyme" (i.e. between 1154 and 1189) the Devonshire manor of Brightley in the parish of Chittlehampton, which he made his seat and where his descendants lived for many generations having adopted "de Brightley" as their surname in lieu of "FitzWarin". They adopted a differenced, simpler, version of the paternal arms, namely Gules, a chief indented argent, which were quartered by their ultimate heirs the Cobley and later Giffard families of Brightley. Surviving images of these de Brightley arms exist on the Giffard monuments in Chittlehampton Church and on the 17th-century escutcheon above the porch of Brightley Barton, and show in addition a mullet gules, a mark of cadency which denotes descent from a third son.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Fulk FitzWarin I於1115年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾廷頓時，他的父親Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz36歲，母親Maud Peverell40歲。他於1139年與Eva de Whittington and Alveston在英國英格蘭史洛普爾廷頓結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1171年在英國格盧斯特斯爾恩波利享年57歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾阿爾波波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Fulk FitzWarin I于1115年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾廷頓时，他的父亲Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz36岁，母亲Maud Peverell40岁。他于1139年与Eva de Whittington and Alveston在英国英格兰史洛普爾廷頓结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1171年在英国格盧斯特斯爾恩波利享年57岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾阿爾波波利。

nameMeanings.name: Fulk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. The name was originally a short form of various compound names containing the word volk ‘people, tribe’ (compare modern English folk). It has gradually died out of general use, but is still used in certain families, in particular the Grevilles. Fulke Greville, 1st Baron Brooke, was a leading figure at the court of Elizabeth I. See also Greville .

variants.name: Fulke
meanings: Of Germanic origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. The name was originally a short form of various compound names containing the word volk ‘people, tribe’ (compare modern English folk). It has gradually died out of general use, but is still used in certain families, in particular the Grevilles. Fulke Greville, 1st Baron Brooke, was a leading figure at the court of Elizabeth I. See also Greville .

en.lifeSummary: When Fulk FitzWarin I was born in 1115, in Whittington, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz, was 36 and his mother, Maud Peverell, was 40. He married Eva de Whittington and Alveston in 1139, in Whittington, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 1171, in Thornbury, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 57, and was buried in Alberbury, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Fulk FitzWarin I est né en 1115, à Whittington, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Maud Peverell, avait 40 ans. Il a épousé Eva de Whittington and Alveston en 1139, à Whittington, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1171, à Thornbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Alberbury, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Fulk FitzWarin I 1115 in Whittington, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz 36 und seine Mutter Maud Peverell 40. Er hat 1139 in Whittington, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom Eva de Whittington and Alveston geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1171 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Thornbury, Gloucestershire, England und wurde in Alberbury, Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Fulk FitzWarin I è nato il nel 1115 nel Whittington, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Maud Peverell, aveva 40 anni. Lui sposato Eva de Whittington and Alveston nel 1139, nel Whittington, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1171, nel Thornbury, Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolto a Alberbury, Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Fulk FitzWarin Iが1115年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアウィッティントンで生まれた時，父Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metzは36歳，母Maud Peverellは40歳であった。1139年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアウィッティントンでEva de Whittington and Alvestonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1171年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアソルンブリーで57歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアアルバーブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Fulk FitzWarin I(이)가 1115년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 휘틴그턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud Peverell(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1139년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 휘틴그턴에서 Eva de Whittington and Alveston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1171년 영국 그루스스터실르 토른버이에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 알버버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Fulk FitzWarin I nasceu em 1115, em Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Maud Peverell, tinha 40 anos. Ele casou-se com Eva de Whittington and Alveston em 1139, em Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1171, em Thornbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Alberbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Fulk FitzWarin I родился в 1115 г. в местности уиттингтон, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Peverell, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Eva de Whittington and Alveston в 1139 г. в местности уиттингтон, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1171 г. в местности таурнбури, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности альбирбури, шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Fulk FitzWarin I nació en 1115, en Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Fulk "The Bold" FitzWarin de Metz, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Maud Peverell, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Eva de Whittington and Alveston en 1139, en Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1171, en Thornbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultado en Alberbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
